topic: warm climate ripe fruit and alcohol

steps.label: cause
steps.text: Black grapes ripen under sustained warm conditions with adequate sunlight.

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: Rapid sugar accumulation raises potential alcohol and advanced flavour development shifts fruit from fresh red tones toward ripe black or dried-fruit character.

steps.label: effect
steps.text: The wine can be fuller-bodied, higher in alcohol and dominated by ripe black fruit, provided water stress and heat are not excessive.

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Formative causal formulation: warm ripening commonly increases sugar, alcohol potential and ripe-fruit character.

_meta.source_note: Inferred formative model - not official WSET assessment or mark-scheme content
